title: thiserror -- Derive Macro for Error Types
description: A derive macro for implementing std::error::Error on custom error enums and structs. Thiserror generates the Display and Error trait implementations from attributes, making it easy to define rich, typed error hierarchies for libraries.

commonPatterns.title: Wrapping multiple error sources
commonPatterns.code:
#[derive(thiserror::Error, Debug)]
enum DataError {
    #[error("Failed to read file")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),

    #[error("Failed to parse JSON")]
    Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),

    #[error("Validation failed: {0}")]
    Validation(String),
}

commonPatterns.title: Transparent error wrapping
Error with backtrace
Combining thiserror (library) with anyhow (application)

whenToUse: In libraries where callers need to match on specific error variants. Also useful in large applications with distinct error domains per module. Use thiserror when you need typed, matchable errors.
alternatives: anyhow (for applications, trait-object errors), snafu (similar but with context selectors), displaydoc (only derives Display). The standard approach is: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications.
